description_long: This object is a small, stylized biplane constructed entirely from light-colored wood, featuring two parallel wings stacked one above the other and supported by vertical struts. The fuselage is cylindrical and tapers toward the tail, which includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal tailplane. At the front, a large, simple wooden propeller is mounted on a rounded nose, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail wheel, all made from the same wood. The cockpit area is minimally detailed, with no visible canopy or instrumentation, suggesting a toy or decorative model rather than a functional aircraft. The surface is smooth and polished, with visible wood grain throughout, giving it a natural, handcrafted appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century biplanes, with a charming, minimalist aesthetic suitable for display or play.
description: A small, light wood biplane with a smooth, polished finish, featuring stacked wings, a simple propeller, and minimal cockpit detail. The design is reminiscent of a handcrafted toy or decorative model.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large wooden propeller, stacked biplane wings, and rounded nose.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the parallel wings, cylindrical fuselage, and tail assembly.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail fin, horizontal stabilizer, and the alignment of the wings.
description_view_3: Side view revealing the full length of the fuselage, propeller, and landing gear arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted propeller; no visible engine detail.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal tailplane.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a single small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted open cockpit with minimal detail, appearing as a single-seat design.
